name: Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop
name_original: Iglesia de Nuestra Señora del Patrocinio, Castillo de Daroca, Neveras – ruta circular
description: This difficult 16.9-mile hike in Sierra de Moncalvillo leads through diverse forests to historic ice pits, offering panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Coloma offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills and mountainous terrain, with significant elevation changes on many routes. The region features a mix of forested areas and open spaces, providing varied scenery for outdoor enthusiasts. Trails often lead through historical areas and offer views of the surrounding natural environment. The varied topography supports a range of hiking experiences, from gentle walks to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Santa Coloma The most popular hiking route is Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop, a 16.9 miles (27.3 km) trail that takes about 8 hours 20 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain, leading through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castroviejo – PiedraHincada(II) loop from Castroviejo, a difficult 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This trail includes significant climbs and offers expansive views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the PiedraHincada(I) – PiedraHincada(II) loop from Castroviejo, a 8.4 miles (13.5 km) trail leading through hilly landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 8 minutes. Hiking around Santa Coloma is defined by its hilly landscapes, forested sections, and historical points of interest. The extensive network of routes offers options for easy strolls, moderate excursions, and challenging mountain hikes. The routes in Santa Coloma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Coloma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa Coloma?
A: There are over 60 hiking routes available around Santa Coloma, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 28 easy trails, 29 moderate routes, and 10 more challenging options, ensuring something for every skill level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Santa Coloma?
A: Hiking around Santa Coloma is characterized by its diverse landscape, featuring rolling hills and mountainous terrain. You'll encounter significant elevation changes on many routes, with a mix of forested areas and open spaces. The region's topography supports everything from gentle strolls to more demanding mountain hikes.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners or families in Santa Coloma?
A: Yes, Santa Coloma offers several easy hikes perfect for beginners or families. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Neveras Ice Pits – LasNeveras loop from Serradero&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 6 km long and features an elevation gain of under 200 meters, making it a pleasant and accessible walk.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Coloma?
A: The trails in Santa Coloma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the challenging ascents, and the expansive views of the surrounding area, particularly on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31820067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castroviejo – PiedraHincada(II) loop from Castroviejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa Coloma?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Santa Coloma are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989685699/moncalvillo-and-las-neveras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 27.3 km route that takes you through varied terrain with substantial elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Santa Coloma?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989685699/moncalvillo-and-las-neveras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.3 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31820067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castroviejo – PiedraHincada(II) loop from Castroviejo&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.4 km path known for its substantial climbs and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or viewpoints to discover along the trails?
A: Yes, the region around Santa Coloma offers several natural attractions and viewpoints. You can explore natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Rocks of Nájera&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Peñuejos Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider routes that lead to summits such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4067711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Moncalvillo&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serradero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa Coloma?
A: The varied terrain of Santa Coloma makes it suitable for hiking across different seasons. While specific recommendations depend on the trail's elevation and exposure, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Some higher elevation routes might be more challenging in winter due to snow, while lower trails remain accessible.

Q: Are there any moderate hikes in Santa Coloma?
A: Absolutely. Santa Coloma has 29 moderate routes. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PiedraHincada(I) – PiedraHincada(II) loop from Castroviejo&lt;/a&gt;, an 13.5 km trail that takes about 4 hours 8 minutes to complete, leading through hilly landscapes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31816948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castroviejo – CocoteDeLaRisca loop from Castroviejo&lt;/a&gt;, covering 10.3 km.

Q: What kind of historical points of interest can be found on hikes in Santa Coloma?
A: Many routes in Santa Coloma lead through areas with historical significance. While specific historical sites vary by trail, the region's hiking experience is often defined by its connection to local history, offering glimpses into past landscapes and traditional uses of the land. The presence of 'Las Neveras' (ice pits) in some route names, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989685699/moncalvillo-and-las-neveras-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop&lt;/a&gt;, suggests historical elements related to ice harvesting.

Q: What should I bring for hiking in Santa Coloma?
A: Given the diverse terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, it's advisable to bring appropriate hiking footwear, sufficient water, and layers of clothing to adapt to changing weather conditions. For longer or more challenging routes, consider bringing snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection. Always check the specific trail conditions before you go.
tips: Located in the center of the urban area, this beautiful church is from the 16th century. The buttresses prop up the church and give the pointed tower a strange feeling of slenderness in keeping with the landscape.
Daroca Castle is a modest peak in the Moncalvillo area separated and isolated from the mountain range where the main peaks are found. The route is an easy and short walk.
Sendero de las Neveras is right next to Las Neveras peak. It is very short and it is worth approaching to see these old constructions.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop trail is considered to be of high difficulty, categorized as 'negra' (black). It involves a substantial elevation gain of approximately 929 meters (3,048 feet) and covers a distance of about 27.3 kilometers (17 miles). The ascent includes ancient cattle trails, indicating potentially rugged or steep sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can typically be accessed from Sojuela, where you might find parking options. The interpretation center, Casa de la Nieve, in Sojuela is a common starting point and would be a good place to inquire about local parking.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, but it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the diverse wildlife and potential for livestock in the area. Remember to pack out any waste.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for hiking the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring snow and ice, making the already difficult trail even more challenging and requiring specialized gear.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for hiking in Sierra de Moncalvillo?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop or other trails within the Sierra de Moncalvillo. However, it's always a good idea to check with local tourism offices for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features several notable points of interest. You'll encounter the historical Las Neveras Ice Pits, a collection of five restored 16th-century ice houses. Other highlights include the Church of Our Lady of Patronage and Daroca Castle.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, winding through extensive forests of pines, oaks, and beeches. As you ascend, the vegetation changes to include poplars, strawberry trees, holm oaks, and hollies. You'll also traverse ravines, such as the Barranco de la Ojosa, and encounter natural springs, which can be seasonal water points.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints with scenic vistas along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, from higher elevations on the trail, you can enjoy spectacular views of Moncalvillo and the peaks of the Sierra del Serradero. You'll also get to see a significant portion of the Ebro Valley in La Rioja, offering expansive panoramic sights.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Moncalvillo and Las Neveras Loop intersects with several other official trails. Notable sections include parts of the CIMA LR09 Moncalvillo routes, the Ruta Dos Dehesas, and the Neveras de Moncalvillo trail. You'll also find sections coinciding with the Sendero de las Neveras and the Sendero al Castillo de Daroca.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on this difficult trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, you should pack essential hiking gear. This includes sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water (especially as natural springs can be seasonal), high-energy snacks, appropriate layers of clothing for changing weather, navigation tools (map, compass, or GPS), a first-aid kit, and sun protection. Trekking poles are also highly recommended for the steep and rugged sections.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of Patronage
